changes_summary: Revised plan to address all 9 flags: (1) Callbacks now pass full group identity {shotId, clipIds, rowId} instead of just shotId. (2) Asset registration bypasses registerGenerationAsset entirely — directly creates AssetRegistryEntry with type:'video/mp4' and calls patchRegistry+registerAsset (matching useStaleVariants pattern). (3) No video duration fetch needed — clip uses shot group time span with from:0/to:duration. (4) Extends existing shotGroupMenu state with clipIds, rowId, hasFinalVideo instead of creating a parallel menu. (5) Context menu pattern confirmed (not panel). (6) 2+ clip threshold confirmed. (7) Unit tests dropped per user note — TypeScript compilation sufficient. (8) Removed the separate useFinalVideoAvailable unit test from success criteria.

success_criteria.criterion: A sky-500 video icon badge appears on shot group overlays when that shot has a final video in shot_final_videos and is not dismissed
Clicking the badge opens the existing shot group context menu with 'Switch to Final Video' and 'Dismiss' items added
'Switch to Final Video' atomically removes all image clips in the shot group and inserts a single video clip spanning the same time range (single applyEdit call)
The replacement video asset is registered with type:'video/mp4' and correct generationId via patchRegistry+registerAsset (not registerGenerationAsset)
'Dismiss' hides the badge for that shot (client-side only, resets on remount)
Callbacks pass full group identity {shotId, clipIds, rowId} — not just shotId
Existing useShotGroups.test.ts tests pass without modification
Badge styling (sky-500) is visually distinct from the amber stale-variant badge
New hook file stays under ~60 lines; no existing file grows by more than ~60 lines
No duplicate menu state — extends existing shotGroupMenu rather than adding parallel state
Manual smoke test: badge appears, switch works, dismiss works, undo restores original clips

assumptions: Dismissed state is client-only (resets on remount) — same pattern as useStaleVariants dismissedAssetKeys. No persistence needed.
The final video clip uses from:0/to:(endTime-startTime) to span the shot group time range. No actual video duration metadata is fetched — the clip is sized to replace the image sequence in-place.
The switch action is atomic (single applyEdit call = single undo step).
Badge only appears on shot groups meeting the existing >=2 clips threshold.
The final video's generationId from shot_final_videos.id is sufficient for asset registration — no variant lookup needed.
useProjectSelectionContext() is available in the TimelineEditor component tree (confirmed in useTimelineState.ts:109).
useShotFinalVideos from the travel tool is safe to import from the video editor — it's a leaf hook with no circular dependencies.
generateUUID and getNextClipId are available in TimelineEditor.tsx scope (used by existing asset management code).
